titleStmt.title: SII 11.64: original edition by C. R. Krishnamacharlu, R. S. Panchamukhi, N. Lakshminaraya Rao and H. K. Narasimhaswami
No. 64. (B.K. No. 100 of 1926-27). MUḶGUND, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT. ON A SLAB IN A FIELD CALLED BEṄKIYAVARA-HOLA ABOUT TWO MILES TO THE NORTH-EAST OF THE VILLAGE.

summary.p: The inscription belongs to the reign of Jayasiṁhadēva (II) and introduces his subordinate Vāvaṇarasa as the governor of the Purigeṟe Three Hundred and Beḷvola Three Hundred provinces. It states that Bhāvaśivadēva was the spiritual head of several temples including Jagadēkamallēśvara constructed by Kundarāja at Muchchuṇḍi in Banavāsi-nāḍu and Śōbhanēśvara built by Śōbhanarasa at Muḷugunda and that his chosen pupil Nāgasvāmin made a gift of gold, land and house-site for certain charities before he started on his pilgrimage to fulfill certain vratas at Gayā, Vāraṇāsi (Benares) etc. Kundarāja mentioned above is evidently the son of (?) Iṟivabeḍaṅga Satyāśraya (Ep. Ind. Vol. XV. p. 330) and Śōbhanarasa is identical with the homonymous governor of the two Six-Hundreds under Taila II in A.D. 982 (Ep. Ind. Vol. IV. p. 206 f). The record is dated Śaka 950, Vibhava, Śrāvaṇa, ba. 8, Wednesday, which corresponds to A.D. 1028, July 17, Wednesday. The month was Adhika-Śrāvaṇa.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
© jayatyāviṣkṛtaṁ viṣṇōrvvārāhaṁ kṣōbhitārṇnavaṁ dakṣiṇōnnatadaṁṣṭrāgraviśrāntabhuvanaṁ vapuḥ ©
© svasti samastabhuvanāśraya śrīpṛthvīvallabha mahārājādhirājaṁ paramēśvaraṁ paramabhaṭṭārakaṁ satyāśra
yakuḷatiḷakaṁ cāḷukyābharaṇaṁ śrīmajjayasiṁhadēvar pṛthvīrājyaṁgeyye tatpādapadmōpajīvi
samadhigatapaṁcamahāśabdaṁ mahāsāmantādhipati mahāpracaṇḍadaṇḍanāyaka śrīmadvāvaṇarasar
beḷvola mūnūṟuṁ purigeṟe mūnūṟumaṁ sarvvābhyantarasiddhiyinda māḷuttamire . yamaniyamāsanaprāṇā
yāmapratyāhāradhyānadhāraṇamaunānuṣṭhānajapasamādhisaṁpannaruṁ kaliyugagāṁgēyaṁ saucāṁjanēyaṁ pratyakṣa
rudraṁ dānasamudraṁ dīnānāthavipravidvajjanacatussameyasamuddharaṇa tapōdhanādhāra nirmmaḷadharmmavistāra nanavarata
dāni daśapratyayajñāni yahōrātrasubhikṣaṁ vibudhajanakaḷpavṛkṣaṁ cakravartti maṇḍaḷika mahāsāmantādyanēkarā
jamaṇḍaḷīmakuṭaphaṭṭitapādāṁbhōjavirājamānarappa śrīmanmuḷugundade sōbhanēśvarada śrībhāvaśivadēvarige
sōbhanēśvaraṁ guṇadabbēśvaraṁ māguṇḍiya lōkabbēśvaramantu mūṟu sthānamuṁ paratiyuṁ kaṇiyuṁ mattaṁ śrījaga
dēkamalladēvargge nivēdisi banavāsināḍoḷe kundarājaṁ muccuṇḍiyoḷmāḍisida jagadēkamallēśvarakke pratibaddhamappa
muccuṇḍi bayala baḷḷigāve keṟepaḷḷi baṁkasariṇne sagaraṇike māvinapaḷḷi sirippaḷḷiyantēḷuṁ grāma mintinituma saThis is also possible to read as jaṁkaṁ.This maysūraṇike also.
rvvanamaśyevāgi dharmmamaṁ pratipāḷisi tapōrājyaṁgeyyuttamiḻdu paramēśvarargge modaloḷ śrī sōbhanarasaṁ . vṛttaṁ © mā
ḍisi sōbhanēśvarama noppise bhāvaśivarpprasiddhiyaṁ muṁdaṇa sōbhana maṭhamaṁ pura maṁ mukhasālemāḍamaṁ mā
ḍisi cakriyoḷpaḍedu śāsanamaṁ banavāsināḍoḷi nārjjisidarintuṭu tanmuni sādhipōtsavaṁ
© Āḷāpaṁ berasanti brahmacaryyamaṁ tāḷdi ḻdu pāḷisidaṁ yaśa maṁ bhūpāḷakaryyetti pogaḷvinaṁ bhāvaśi
var © Enisi negaḷda bhāvaśivadēva rkkiṟiya māṇiyaṁ kaikoṇḍu pōṣisi maduviyaṁ māḍi naḍepida dhammasantetiya
brāhmaṇasarvvadharmmādhikāri brahmapurimukhyaṁ śrīmannāgasvāmi dharmmagurugaḷalli javaḷiguḷavaṁ
mannaṇe pratipattiverasu brahmapurige kēriyu keyyumaṁ śrīguṇadabbēśvaraṁ śrī sōbhanēśvara manteraḍuṁ purada sāsirvva
ruṁ purassararāgi paḍedu paramēśvararaṁ kanyādānada brāhmaṇōttama viśēṣadi ḻdavargge bharaṇaṁgeye traipu
ruṣadēvaraṁ pratiṣṭhemāḍi dēvālayamuṁ maṭhamumenisi yāśramamaṁ māḍisi mattaṁ palavuṁ kanyādānaṁ hiraṇya
dānaṁ bhūmidāna māhāradāna mudakadānaṁ bhaiṣajyadāna meṁbinitaṟoḷaṁ mahādāniyenisi brahmaṇyadoḷaṁ
sucāritranuṁ suciyu magnihōtrādikṛyāratanumāgi palavukālaṁ sukhadiṁdamire © sakavarṣa 950 ttane
ya vibhava saṁvatsarada śrāvaṇa bahuḷa Aṣṭami budhavāradandu śrībhāvaśivadēva rttavage muktikālamappuda naṟi
du munna meṁṭeneya divasadandu prajāparivāremaṁ nerapi cellapayyaṅgaḷgaṁ śrībhāvaśivargge Anukramadiṁ sthāna
rājyabharamaṁ koṭṭu tamage parōkṣavinayaṁ gaṅgāsnānamuṁ gayeyoḷpiṇḍapradānamuṁ vāraṇāsiyoḷdēvāla
ya mintinitumaṁ mārppantu nāgasvāmi bēḍikoṇḍu samarppaṇe māḍe yavaruṁ gurugaḷa be
sanuma nanēkavrataṅgaḷmaṁ kaikoṇḍu tīrtthayātrege pōguttuṁ dharmmamēkaṁ paraṁ sakhā Eṁbuda naṟivudaṟiṁ dartthamaṁ
sarvvasvanivēdanaṁ geydu vṛttiyoḷkiṟidaṁ santatinimitta mirisi mikkudellamaṁ dēvarggaṁ maṭhakkaṁ satrakkaṁ vidyādā
nādigaḷgellamāgi koṭṭa poṅgadyāṇa vaṟuvattu 60 keymatta rppanneraḍu 12 mane nivēsana vondu 1 © Intini
tumaṁ modalaṁ kiḍalīyede vṛddhiyoḷe dharmamaṁ tamma santatiyava rnnaḍeyisuvarkkiḍisadantu brahma
3 After this there is a blank space for four or five letters.

puriya mahājanamuṁ sāsirvvaruṁ parirakṣisuva rupēkṣegeyyadava rttāve māḍida dātāra rappar
© mattaṁ kēriya mahājanakke māḍidda sameyamentene Illiya keyyuṁ maneyuṁ jēṣṭhanorvvanoḍave
mikkudellaṁ mūvattircchāsira sameyaṁ pūrvada bharaṇigara santatiyallade peṟargge keymaneyaṁ māṟalā
gadu pūrvvigarādoḍa mārorvvaronde geydu sāmyama nāḷvudu . sthānadācāryyarggaṁ Eraḍuṁkēriya sāsi
rvvarggaṁ nirvvikalpadiṁ sakhāyarāgi baḷivandu bāḷvudu . mane sarvvanamaśyaṁ kayge sāsirvvaraṁ nōḍi tiṟuvudintī
mārggamaṁ tappade naḍeda brāhmaṇanuṁ dharmmamaṁ pāḷisi dācāryyaruṁ sāsirvvaru manantamappa puṇyapha
lama neyduvar © svadattāṁ paradattāṁ vā yō harēta vasundharāṁ ṣaṣṭirvvarṣasahasrāṇi viṣṭhāyāṁ jāyatē kri
